concerns.description: Images missing or broken
concerns.analysis: Several meaningful data visualizations in the 'STATE-MANAGED SPECIES – KINGFISHES' section are missing or incorrectly rendered. Specifically, the charts for Figures 6, 10, and 12 have been erroneously replaced by cropped images of the page footers (containing only the page number). Furthermore, the charts for Figures 5, 9, and 11 are missing completely from the HTML, represented only by text placeholders like '[Image: A line chart...]'. This results in a critical loss of information.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF page 173 (Document page 170) contains Figure 6, a line chart showing the annual proportion of adults.
concerns.output_quote: The corresponding <figure> in the HTML contains an image of the page footer instead of the chart, evidenced by the alt text: 'The number 170 is in the lower center. Two dark blue buttons are on the right side...'

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (433): Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=433, pages_affected=344/350. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains legislative text where deletions are indicated by strikethrough and additions are indicated by underline. The extraction pipeline failed to process the strikethrough formatting and instead extracted the deleted text as regular, active text. This alters the legal meaning of the document by presenting repealed regulations as if they were current. For example, on page 204, an entire block of text meant for a sign is struck out in the PDF, but appears as a normal paragraph in
concerns.source_pdf_quote: ATTENTION: THESE WATERS MAY BE CONTAMINATED BY HUMAN OR ANIMAL WASTE. SWIMMING IS NOT ADVISED IN THESE WATERS BECAUSE OF THE INCREASED RISK OF ILLNESS. OFFICE OF THE STATE HEALTH DIRECTOR. [Note: This entire text block has a strikethrough line drawn through it in the PDF]
concerns.output_quote: "text": "ATTENTION: THESE WATERS MAY BE CONTAMINATED BY HUMAN OR ANIMAL WASTE. SWIMMING IS NOT ADVISED IN THESE WATERS BECAUSE OF THE INCREASED RISK OF ILLNESS. OFFICE OF THE STATE HEALTH DIRECTOR."

concerns.description: Table structure corrupted
concerns.analysis: An examination of the 'Eligibility Pool Board Meeting Summary' table (which spans across multiple pages) reveals a column merging error. On PDF page 182 (JSON page 185), the data rows incorrectly span the 'TOTAL REVIEWED' value across two columns, effectively swallowing the adjacent empty 'INCOMP.' column. For example, in the row for '10/4/2006', the PDF shows the value '21' under 'TOTAL REVIEWED' and a blank space under 'INCOMP.'. The JSON output merges these by giving the '21' cell a `num_colu
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Row for '10/4/2006' with '21' in the 'TOTAL REVIEWED' column and a blank space in the 'INCOMP.' column.
concerns.location: Page 185, 'Eligibility Pool Board Meeting Summary' table

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly
concerns.analysis: A review of the list structures reveals a hierarchy flattening error in the text for rule '15A NCAC 03R .0105 SPECIAL SECONDARY NURSERY AREAS'. In the source PDF, item '(2) In the Pamlico and Pungo rivers Area:' acts as a parent to several indented sub-items (a through e, such as '(a) Pungo Creek'). In the JSON output, these sub-items are extracted as top-level siblings to item (2), rather than being nested as its children. This flattens the list hierarchy and corrupts the structural relationshi
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
(2) In the Pamlico and Pungo rivers Area: 
(a) Pungo Creek - west of a line beginning...
concerns.output_quote: {"text": "In the Pamlico and Pungo rivers Area:"} (with no children array), followed by sibling {"text": "(a) Pungo Creek - west of a line beginning..."}

concerns.description: Content visually duplicated on page
concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains a clear instance of content duplication where a full document page is embedded as an image and immediately followed by the extracted text of that exact same page. A letter from Stephen W. Murphey to Gregg Waugh is rendered first as an image tag, and then the full text of the letter ('ROY COOPER...', 'Dear Gregg...', etc.) is printed as HTML paragraphs directly beneath it. This forces the user to encounter the same content twice.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: PDF page 24 (Document page 21) shows a formal letter dated March 1, 2019.
concerns.output_quote:
The HTML includes an image tag `` immediately followed by the extracted text: `

ROY COOPER
Governor

MICHAEL S. REGAN
Secretary...`

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (3 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=3. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 82 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=82, examples=['https://files.nc.gov/ncdeq/051420-mfc-meeting/03-ChairmanRep', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/05-2020-briefing-book', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_file?p_l_id=']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: 20 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: https://www.ncsbe.gov/Ethics/Education; https://EFILE.ncsbe.gov/; https://ncdenrits.webex.com/ncdenrits/onstage/g.php?MTID=ea8608d0638d06136715b7a; http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/mfc-proposed-rules.*; http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/shellfish-closure-maps.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=20. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table cells contain 27 unlinked URL(s)/email(s) that should be hyperlinks
concerns.analysis: bare_urls_in_tables=27. Examples: p196: https://ncdenrits.webex.com/ncdenrits/onstage/g.ph; p198: molly.masich@oah.nc.gov; p198: molly.masich@oah.nc.gov. URLs and emails in table cells should be clickable links.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 2 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=2. Examples: 6 item(s): "† The schedule assumes no rulemaking is required t"; 1 item(s): "*Fisheries Director no longer has authority to ope". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Links displaced to end of page: 11 link(s) clustered at bottom of 4 page(s) instead of inline in text
concerns.analysis: displaced_link_pages=4, total_displaced=11. Links appear as standalone elements at the end of pages rather than being inline in the paragraph text.

concerns.description: Visual emphasis loss
concerns.analysis: The source PDF uses underline formatting extensively to denote new additions to regulatory text. The extraction JSON does not capture or represent this underline formatting, resulting in a loss of visual emphasis that is critical for understanding legislative changes.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: WARNING! SEWAGE TREATMENT EFFLUENT DISCHARGE SITE. SWIMMING IS NOT ADVISED IN THESE WATERS BECAUSE OF THE INCREASED RISK OF ILLNESS. OFFICE OF THE STATE HEALTH DIRECTOR. [Note: This text is underlined in the PDF]
concerns.output_quote: "text": "WARNING! SEWAGE TREATMENT EFFLUENT DISCHARGE SITE. SWIMMING IS NOT ADVISED IN THESE WATERS BECAUSE OF THE INCREASED RISK OF ILLNESS. OFFICE OF THE STATE HEALTH DIRECTOR."

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 43 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=43. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 32 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 21: "Klibansky, Lara"; page 21: "Gillikin, Dana"; page 63: "Submitted"; page 63: "Name:"; page 63: "City:"
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=32. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.
